title: Is polyester clothing affecting our fertility/hormones?

chunks.title: Introduction to Polyester and Human Health
chunks.summary: The speaker begins by discussing skepticism about the impact of polyester clothing on human health, particularly fertility. They reference studies on dogs that suggest synthetic fabrics might negatively affect hormone levels and reproductive health.
chunks.transcript: let's start with polyester clothing and whether this is a problem for humans or not i was originally skeptical i thought how can polyester be an issue for humans and to be fair there are no studies in humans but there are some very interesting studies in dogs which i will talk about in a moment which suggests that slinging your testicles or covering your lady parts in polyester clothing or underwear might not be a good thing for both male or female hormones and fertility

chunks.title: Personal Experience with Natural Fabrics
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their personal journey of eliminating synthetic fabrics from their wardrobe, opting for natural options like cotton. They express a desire to create a line of natural clothing products and discuss the comfort of wearing cotton in a tropical environment.
chunks.transcript: i didn't believe it when i first saw it but it's interesting and it's compelling and it makes sense evolutionarily when we have had such synthetic fabrics next to our skin so increasingly i have been trying to eliminate all synthetic fabrics from my life to the point that i'm wearing only cotton shorts i'm in costa rica most of the time i don't have to wear underwear because what's the point of underwear when you're in costa rica and you just want to jump in the pool anyway and when i'm jumping in my pool i can wear cotton shorts which you guys have seen on my videos and you always ask what kind of shorts i wear if you guys really want i will build we will build a company that makes natural clothing natural swimwear natural loungewear natural shorts then i will recreate something like the shorts i have the shorts i have are just cotton there's nothing special about them but if you want something special we can do this but only if you guys enjoy this or you really some something that you really are asking for but regardless in my own life i wear cotton shorts the majority of the time this shirt which says kale is this is the new version of the kills shirts guys these will be available soon on thecarnivoremd.com website this is the version probably 1.5 the version 2.0 is coming soon but kayla's shirts are coming soon you better believe they're all cotton to the point that i have changed my surf wear my bathing suits and i'm working on this because i'll tell you this much cotton bathing suits cotton board shorts don't exist i use my sort of short cut cotton shorts to surf in now and they work okay but i'm doing an experiment to see if i feel any differently and version 2.0 of the cotton shorts required that i cut the back pocket off because that was catching water when i was surfing and delaying me as i was trying to paddle back out so i have a pair of cotton shorts that are my surf shorts now that i cut the back pocket off but i want to

chunks.title: Study on Textiles and Female Dogs
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to a specific study from 2008 on the effects of different textiles on conception in female dogs. The findings indicated that dogs wearing polyester underwear experienced hormonal changes affecting their reproductive health.
chunks.transcript: first down the rabbit hole so this is from journal of obstetrics and gynecology in 2008 and the title is an experimental study of the effect of different types of textiles on conception so these were dogs 35 female dogs divided into five equal groups four tests and one control each one of the four test groups was dressed in one type of textile underpants made from either 100 polyester 50 polyester 50 cotton 100 cotton or 100 wool the pants were worn for 12 months so these are female dogs dogs were followed during this period and for six months after the removal of the pants by measuring serum estradiol 17 beta estradiol and uh oh just serum 17 beta estradiol and progesterone and by mating the electrostatic potentials were also measured on the textile covered skin eight female dogs wearing polyester coating containing textile showed diminished serum progesterone in the estrus of the oestrous cycle i have to do a little research on what this terminology means in dogs but i imagine this is the ovulation period or perhaps the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle when at least human women have increased progesterone so they had decreased progesterone in the luteal phase presumably this is what this is talking about that's not good because if you don't have progesterone in the luteal phase then the corpus luteum will not be able to implant and will not be able to stay in the uterus and await sperm for insemination or conception and they go on to say they did not conceive on mating or insemination five months after the pants had been removed the serum progesterone of eight of the dogs had normalized and they can see the electrostatic potentials were detected on the skin of all dogs dressed in polyester containing textiles so it changed the electrostatic potential of the dogs in polyester containing textile the low serum progesterone and non-conception of the eight dogs could point to an ovulation and failure of luteinization which is what i was talking about with progesterone it is suggested that the electrostatic potentials detected on the skin create an electrostatic field that inhibits ovarian function however the effect proved to be reversible so i

chunks.title: Impact on Male Dogs' Reproductive Health
chunks.summary: Continuing the research theme, the speaker introduces a 1993 study that examined the impact of textile fabrics on male dogs' spermatogenesis. Results showed that polyester clothing had detrimental effects on sperm count and quality, highlighting a potential fertility issue.
chunks.transcript: going on there i don't know i don't think the polyester fabrics or polyester determinants or derivatives are moving across the skin but is this creating some sort of electrostatic potential that is changing the way the ovaries function it sounds so far-fetched but that is a really interesting study and then look at this one in male dogs so the effects of different types of textile fabric on spermatogenesis this is from your urology research in 1993 so essentially the same thing they had dogs male dogs they fashioned underwear to fit loosely in the scrotal area so as to avoid an insulating effect worn continuously for 24 months during which the semen character testicular temperature hormones including serum testosterone fsh lh and prolactin fsh and lhr um and prolactin as well these are pituitary-derived hormones fsh and lh signal to the testicles please refer to the previous episode i've done on how to optimize your testosterone for information about how the human male pituitary gonadal axis works and they also did a testicular biopsy the garment was then removed investigations repeated through another 12 months and what they found in the polyester group the testicular temperature showed insignificant changes during the period the pants were worn so it wasn't heating the balls which we know is a bad thing for spermatogenesis by the end of 24 months there was a significant decrease in sperm count and modal sperm with an increase in abnormal forms the p value is less than .001 wow okay testicular biopsy showed degenerative changes after garment removal the semen character improved gradually to normal in 10 dogs two remained oligozoospermic meaning they had less than normal amounts of sperm insignificant changes in hormones during the study in contrast cotton and control groups showed insignificant changes in the aforementioned parameters during the 36 months of the study that would be the sperm count and modal sperm and abnormal forms of sperm so the polyester pans in conclusion had a deleterious effect on somatogenesis in the dogs which was reversible in the majority of cases the

chunks.title: Reversible Effects and Concerns for Humans
chunks.summary: The speaker notes that while the negative effects observed in dogs were often reversible after removing polyester garments, there are concerns about similar impacts on human reproduction. They question the prevalence of polyester in women's underwear and workout clothes.
chunks.transcript: assume that the electrostatic potentials generated by the polyester fabric play a role in it really fascinating stuff here enough to convince me to do a zero polyester on my balls experiment for quite a while not necessarily trying to make any babies at this moment in my life but i do have concerns about improper formation of sperm in my testicles and what about for women are they not having proper amounts of luteinization or in the ludial phase could this affect conception how much

chunks.title: Experiment with Cotton Clothing
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses a personal experiment where they surf in cotton shorts, noting the practicality and comfort, despite minor issues like water retention. They express an ongoing interest in finding or creating better natural fabric options for various activities.
chunks.transcript: the majority from what i understand cotton underwear in women is sometimes used but when i ask my girlfriend she says most of her stuff is polyester and when i see her in workout clothes almost all of his polyester so there's a real need for this here so this is all to say that i've done this hilarious experiment this week that i documented on instagram and i've been surfing in cotton shorts it works great except for the fact that i had to cut the back part of the shorts off because the cot the pocket was catching the water and the fact that they don't dry very quickly so ideally in the future i will be able to create maybe some wool board shorts or wool surfing shorts but i don't want polyester fabrics on my

chunks.title: A Call for Awareness on Synthetic Fabrics
chunks.summary: Concluding the video, the speaker emphasizes the prevalence of synthetic textiles in everyday life and their potential health impacts. They invite viewers to consider whether polyester and other synthetic materials might be affecting their health negatively.
chunks.transcript: i don't want polyester fabrics on shirts i don't want polyester fabrics in my bedding and i've gone so far as to this is also because i like the way this mattress works i have a fully wool mattress 100 wool mattress it's fully natural textiles there's nothing synthetic in the mattress either so that is all quite fascinating to me i want all natural fabrics just to see how it affects the way that i feel in my life but as you guys know we are surrounded by toxins we are surrounded by textiles that are synthetic and just wanted to raise this as a question do you think that the polyester in your life could be affecting your life negatively it's quite fascinating and i'll keep going down the rabbit hole for you guys if you want me to but hopefully that is
